controls.name: AI Governance Structure
controls.description: Establish organizational governance structures for AI including board oversight, AI ethics committees, designated roles, and clear decision-making authority.
keyActivities: Define AI governance charter and mandate
Establish AI ethics committee or board
Assign roles: AI officer, ethics lead, risk owner
Set governance review cadence

maturity.basic: Ad hoc AI governance, no formal structure
maturity.mature: Formal AI governance committee with defined charter and regular meetings
maturity.advanced: Integrated AI governance across enterprise with automated compliance tracking

controls.name: AI Policy Framework
controls.description: Develop and maintain comprehensive AI policies covering acceptable use, ethical boundaries, risk appetite, and compliance requirements.
keyActivities: Draft AI acceptable use policy
Define ethical boundaries and red lines
Establish AI risk appetite statement
Implement policy review and update cycle

maturity.basic: Generic IT policies with no AI-specific provisions
maturity.mature: Dedicated AI policy suite aligned with organizational risk appetite
maturity.advanced: Dynamic policy framework that adapts to regulatory changes and emerging risks

controls.name: AI Risk Assessment
controls.description: Conduct systematic risk assessments for AI systems covering technical, ethical, legal, and societal risks throughout the lifecycle.
keyActivities: Identify AI-specific risks (bias, drift, adversarial attacks)
Assess likelihood and impact of identified risks
Map risks to controls and mitigations
Review and update risk assessments periodically

maturity.basic: No formal AI risk assessment process
maturity.mature: Structured AI risk assessment integrated into project lifecycle
maturity.advanced: Continuous automated risk monitoring with real-time dashboards

controls.name: AI Risk Classification
controls.description: Classify AI systems by risk level to determine applicable governance requirements, regulatory obligations, and control intensity.
keyActivities: Define risk classification criteria and tiers
Assess each AI system against classification criteria
Document classification rationale and maintain register
Trigger appropriate controls based on classification

maturity.basic: No risk classification for AI systems
maturity.mature: Formal risk tiering with documented criteria aligned to EU AI Act categories
maturity.advanced: Automated classification with continuous reassessment as systems evolve

controls.name: Data Quality Governance
controls.description: Ensure training, validation, and testing datasets meet quality standards including completeness, accuracy, representativeness, and freedom from bias.
keyActivities: Define data quality criteria and metrics
Assess training data for representativeness and bias
Implement data validation and cleansing pipelines
Document data provenance and lineage

maturity.basic: Ad hoc data quality checks
maturity.mature: Systematic data quality framework with automated validation pipelines
maturity.advanced: Continuous data quality monitoring with drift detection and auto-remediation

controls.name: Data Privacy & Protection
controls.description: Implement technical and organizational measures to protect personal data used in AI systems, ensuring compliance with data protection laws.
keyActivities: Conduct data protection impact assessments (DPIAs)
Implement data minimization and purpose limitation
Apply anonymization and pseudonymization techniques
Ensure lawful basis for data processing

maturity.basic: Basic data handling without AI-specific privacy controls
maturity.mature: Comprehensive DPIAs for AI systems with privacy-by-design implementation
maturity.advanced: Privacy-enhancing technologies (federated learning, differential privacy) integrated

controls.name: Model Documentation
controls.description: Maintain comprehensive documentation of AI models including purpose, architecture, training data, performance metrics, limitations, and intended use.
keyActivities: Create model cards for each AI system
Document training methodology and hyperparameters
Record performance metrics and benchmark results
Maintain version history and change log

maturity.basic: Minimal model documentation
maturity.mature: Standardized model cards with comprehensive technical documentation
maturity.advanced: Automated documentation generation integrated with ML pipelines

controls.name: AI System Testing & Validation
controls.description: Conduct rigorous testing and validation of AI systems before deployment, including functional, performance, fairness, safety, and adversarial testing.
keyActivities: Define test plans covering accuracy, fairness, robustness
Execute pre-deployment validation protocols
Perform adversarial testing and red-teaming
Document test results and acceptance criteria

maturity.basic: Basic accuracy testing only
maturity.mature: Comprehensive test suite including fairness, robustness, and edge cases
maturity.advanced: Continuous automated testing with adversarial simulation and chaos engineering

controls.name: Deployment Validation & Approval
controls.description: Implement formal approval gates and validation checks before AI systems are deployed to production environments.
keyActivities: Define deployment readiness criteria
Conduct pre-deployment review and sign-off
Validate production environment compatibility
Implement canary/staged deployment practices

maturity.basic: No formal deployment approval process
maturity.mature: Formal deployment gates with multi-stakeholder sign-off
maturity.advanced: Automated deployment pipelines with integrated compliance checks

controls.name: AI Transparency & Disclosure
controls.description: Ensure transparent disclosure when AI is used in decision-making, including purpose, capabilities, limitations, and the role of AI in outcomes.
keyActivities: Disclose AI use in customer-facing decisions
Publish AI system purposes and capabilities
Communicate AI limitations to stakeholders
Maintain public AI system register where required

maturity.basic: No disclosure of AI use
maturity.mature: Proactive disclosure with standardized transparency reports
maturity.advanced: Real-time transparency dashboards with stakeholder-accessible explanations

controls.name: Explainability Mechanism
controls.description: Implement mechanisms that provide understandable explanations of AI decisions to affected individuals, proportionate to the risk level.
keyActivities: Select appropriate explainability techniques per use case
Implement local and global explanation methods
Provide user-accessible explanations in plain language
Enable contestation of AI-driven decisions

maturity.basic: Black-box models with no explainability
maturity.mature: Explainability techniques applied with user-accessible explanations
maturity.advanced: Multi-modal explanations with interactive exploration and counterfactuals

controls.name: Human Oversight Mechanism
controls.description: Implement appropriate human oversight mechanisms (human-in-the-loop, human-on-the-loop, or human-in-command) proportionate to the AI system's risk level.
keyActivities: Determine appropriate oversight level per risk classification
Design human intervention points in AI workflows
Train operators on oversight responsibilities
Implement override and stop mechanisms

maturity.basic: No formal human oversight of AI decisions
maturity.mature: Risk-proportionate human oversight with defined intervention protocols
maturity.advanced: Dynamic oversight levels that adjust based on system confidence and context

controls.name: Bias Detection & Mitigation
controls.description: Systematically detect, measure, and mitigate bias in AI systems across the development lifecycle, from data collection through deployment.
keyActivities: Define fairness metrics relevant to use case
Test for bias across protected characteristics
Apply pre-processing, in-processing, or post-processing debiasing
Monitor for bias drift in production

maturity.basic: No bias testing performed
maturity.mature: Systematic bias testing with defined fairness metrics and mitigation strategies
maturity.advanced: Continuous bias monitoring with automated alerts and mitigation pipelines

controls.name: Fairness Impact Assessment
controls.description: Assess the potential impact of AI systems on different demographic groups, identifying risks of discrimination or exclusion before deployment.
keyActivities: Identify affected demographic groups
Assess differential impact across groups
Document fairness trade-offs and decisions
Engage affected communities in assessment

maturity.basic: No fairness impact assessment
maturity.mature: Structured fairness impact assessments with stakeholder input
maturity.advanced: Participatory assessments with ongoing community feedback loops

controls.name: AI Security Testing
controls.description: Test AI systems for security vulnerabilities including adversarial attacks, data poisoning, model extraction, and prompt injection.
keyActivities: Conduct adversarial robustness testing
Test for data poisoning vulnerabilities
Assess model extraction and inversion risks
Test for prompt injection (GenAI systems)

maturity.basic: Standard cybersecurity testing only, no AI-specific tests
maturity.mature: AI-specific security testing covering adversarial, poisoning, and extraction attacks
maturity.advanced: Continuous adversarial testing with automated red-teaming and defense adaptation

controls.name: AI System Access Control
controls.description: Implement access controls for AI systems, models, training data, and inference endpoints to prevent unauthorized access or manipulation.
keyActivities: Define access control policies for AI assets
Implement role-based access to models and data
Secure inference endpoints and APIs
Monitor and audit access patterns

maturity.basic: Basic access controls inherited from IT infrastructure
maturity.mature: AI-specific access controls with role-based policies and audit trails
maturity.advanced: Zero-trust architecture for AI assets with real-time access anomaly detection

controls.name: AI Audit & Assurance
controls.description: Conduct internal and external audits of AI systems to verify compliance with governance standards, ethical principles, and regulatory requirements.
keyActivities: Define AI audit scope and methodology
Conduct periodic internal AI audits
Engage third-party auditors for independent assurance
Maintain audit trail and evidence repository

maturity.basic: No AI-specific auditing
maturity.mature: Regular internal audits with documented findings and remediation tracking
maturity.advanced: Continuous audit automation with third-party assurance and public reporting

controls.name: Responsibility Assignment
controls.description: Clearly assign and document responsibilities for AI system outcomes, including development, deployment, monitoring, and incident response roles.
keyActivities: Define RACI matrix for AI system lifecycle
Assign accountability for AI system outcomes
Document responsibility chains for incident escalation
Review and update assignments as systems evolve

maturity.basic: Unclear or undocumented AI responsibilities
maturity.mature: Formal RACI matrices with clear accountability chains
maturity.advanced: Dynamic responsibility assignment integrated with organizational change management

controls.name: AI Incident Response
controls.description: Establish procedures for detecting, reporting, and responding to AI system incidents, malfunctions, and unintended harmful outcomes.
keyActivities: Define AI incident classification and severity levels
Establish incident detection and escalation procedures
Create incident response playbooks for AI-specific scenarios
Conduct post-incident reviews and implement improvements

maturity.basic: AI incidents handled through general IT incident process
maturity.mature: Dedicated AI incident response process with specialized playbooks
maturity.advanced: Automated incident detection with predictive alerting and self-healing capabilities

controls.name: Continuous Monitoring
controls.description: Implement ongoing monitoring of AI system performance, fairness, drift, and compliance in production environments.
keyActivities: Define monitoring metrics and thresholds
Implement model performance and data drift monitoring
Monitor for fairness degradation over time
Set up automated alerts for anomalous behaviour

maturity.basic: No ongoing monitoring of AI systems
maturity.mature: Comprehensive monitoring dashboards with alerting for key metrics
maturity.advanced: Predictive monitoring with automated drift correction and retraining triggers

controls.name: Human Rights Impact Assessment
controls.description: Assess the potential impact of AI systems on fundamental human rights, dignity, and societal well-being before and during deployment.
keyActivities: Identify fundamental rights potentially affected
Assess proportionality and necessity of AI use
Engage with affected communities and civil society
Document mitigations and residual risks to rights

maturity.basic: No human rights assessment for AI systems
maturity.mature: Structured human rights impact assessments for high-risk AI systems
maturity.advanced: Ongoing rights monitoring with stakeholder advisory panels and public reporting

controls.name: Societal & Environmental Impact
controls.description: Evaluate the broader societal and environmental impact of AI systems, including effects on employment, social cohesion, and sustainability.
keyActivities: Assess impact on employment and workforce
Evaluate environmental footprint of AI training and operation
Consider effects on social cohesion and democratic processes
Engage with diverse stakeholders on societal implications

maturity.basic: No consideration of broader AI impacts
maturity.mature: Structured societal impact assessments with environmental considerations
maturity.advanced: Comprehensive ESG integration with AI sustainability metrics and public reporting
